skill.name: audit
skill.description: Perform comprehensive audit of interface quality across accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design. Generates detailed report of issues with severity ratings and recommendations.
skill.summary: Systematic UI quality auditing for accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design issues.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced 16 false positive findings. The 13 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are misidentified YAML frontmatter description fields. The 3 'external_commands' flags are documentation examples showing command usage syntax, not actual code execution. This skill is a markdown instruction file for UI auditing with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Poor interface quality damages user experience and excludes users with disabilities. This skill provides systematic auditing across accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design to identify issues before they reach production.
actual_capabilities: Scan for accessibility issues including contrast ratios, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and semantic HTML
Identify performance problems like layout thrashing, expensive animations, and missing optimizations
Detect theming issues including hard-coded colors and broken dark mode variants
Check responsive design for fixed widths, touch targets, and missing breakpoints
Identify AI-generated design anti-patterns and quality tells
Generate prioritized reports with severity ratings and fix recommendations
limitations: Cannot automatically fix identified issues - only documents them for other commands to address
Relies on visual inspection and pattern matching rather than automated testing tools
Cannot measure actual runtime performance metrics or Lighthouse scores
Requires human verification for accessibility findings before treating as confirmed

use_cases.title: Pre-release Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Run comprehensive audit before shipping to catch accessibility and quality issues early

use_cases.title: Design System Compliance Check
use_cases.description: Verify components adhere to design tokens and theming standards across the codebase
use_cases.target_user: Design system maintainers and UI engineers

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audit for WCAG
use_cases.description: Systematic review of accessibility barriers and WCAG compliance gaps

prompt_templates.title: Basic Accessibility Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the accessibility of the current component. Check for contrast issues, missing ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation problems, and semantic HTML issues. Generate a report with severity ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the performance characteristics of this interface. Look for layout thrashing, expensive animations, missing lazy loading, and render optimization opportunities. Document findings with recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Full Quality Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive audit across all dimensions: accessibility, performance, theming, responsive design, and anti-patterns. Generate a prioritized report with executive summary, detailed findings by severity, and recommended commands for fixes.

prompt_templates.title: AI Design Tell Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface for AI-generated design anti-patterns. Check for generic AI color palettes, gradient text, glassmorphism, hero metrics, card grids, and other tells. Provide a pass/fail verdict with specific evidence.

output: ANTI-PATTERNS VERDICT: PASS - No AI-generated design tells detected
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: 7 issues found (2 critical, 3 high, 2 medium). Top concerns: missing form labels and insufficient contrast on error states.
CRITICAL: Password input lacks associated label - screen readers cannot identify purpose. Add aria-label or linked label element. WCAG 1.3.1 violation.
HIGH: Error state contrast ratio is 3.2:1, below 4.5:1 minimum. Users with low vision may not see validation errors.
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY: 5 issues found (1 high, 2 medium, 2 low). Primary concern: layout thrashing in animation loop.
HIGH: Animation loop reads offsetHeight then modifies style.height in same frame. Causes forced synchronous layouts. Use FLIP technique or animate transform instead.
MEDIUM: Chart images lack loading='lazy' attribute. Above-fold content should prioritize, below-fold charts should lazy-load.

best_practices: Start with anti-patterns verdict to catch AI-generated design tells before detailed analysis
Document impact (why it matters) for every finding, not just the issue location
Prioritize ruthlessly - not everything can be critical, focus on what actually affects users
anti_patterns: Reporting issues without explaining user impact or which standard is violated
Mixing severity levels inconsistently or marking everything as critical
Skipping positive findings that celebrate what works well in the codebase

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix the issues it finds?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on thorough documentation and diagnosis. Use complementary commands like /normalize, /optimize, or /harden to address specific issue categories after the audit completes.

faq.question: What accessibility standards does this check against?
faq.answer: The audit references WCAG 2.1 guidelines, checking for Level A (critical), AA (high priority), and AAA (medium priority) compliance. Findings include specific WCAG criterion references where applicable.

faq.question: How do I know which issues to fix first?
faq.answer: The report organizes findings by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and includes a prioritized recommendations section. Start with Critical issues that block core functionality or violate WCAG A.

faq.question: Can I audit only specific areas instead of everything?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass an 'area' argument to focus the audit on specific features like 'navigation', 'forms', or 'dashboard'. The audit will concentrate on that area while still checking all quality dimensions.

faq.question: What makes a finding 'Critical' vs 'High' severity?
faq.answer: Critical issues block core functionality or violate WCAG Level A (legal compliance risk). High severity issues significantly impact usability or violate WCAG AA. Critical requires immediate fix before shipping.

faq.question: Should I report every minor issue I find?
faq.answer: No. Too many low-priority findings creates noise and reduces report value. Focus on issues that actually matter to users. Document patterns and systemic problems rather than every isolated inconsistency.
